new_classes.definition_en: A grape of the Glera vine variety (Vitis vinifera cv. Glera), the primary grape variety used in the production of Prosecco PDO sparkling wine.

A geographic region recognised under EU law as a protected designation of origin for Prosecco, including 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', and 'Asolo — Prosecco'.
A quality borne by a wine or other agricultural product certifying that it holds a protected designation of origin (PDO) status under EU law.

A sparkling wine with a protected designation of origin (PDO), produced from grapes of the Glera vine variety harvested in one of the four recognised Prosecco PDO regions in north-east Italy: 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', or 'Asolo — Prosecco'. Classified under CN code 2204 10 15.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the four key distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) Prosecco is a sparkling wine (subClassOf SparklingWine); (2) it bears a PDO quality (someValuesFrom ProtectedDesignationOfOriginQuality); (3) it is produced from Glera grapes (someValuesFrom via hasGrapeVariety GleraGrape); (4) the grapes must originate from one of the four named PDO regions (someValuesFrom via hasGrapeOrigin ProseccoDesignationRegion). A small coverage gap remains because the four individual named PDO regions ('Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', 'Asolo — Prosecco') are modelled as a single class ProseccoDesignationRegion rather than as four named individuals with a hasValue restriction — the closed enumeration of exactly these four regions cannot be fully expressed without introducing named individuals or a union of subclasses. This accounts for the 0.08 deduction.
